meta.setting: Creek fords, gravel bars, wheel ruts, cold water, fish runs, and washed tracks below the wagon roads
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with travel risk, water judgment, tracking pressure, and local route knowledge

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Respectful grounding for Wintu presence and long relationship with lands and waterways in the region.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Deer
research_grounding.use: Wildlife movement and tracks around creek corridors.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Creek Fords region page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H3 local marker line in the register of Gravel Bar, Wheel Rut, and Cold Water
square image reference immediately after the marker line
opening blockquote built as three short generated lines: ford object, water or track detail, plain table meaning
two compact paragraphs establishing fords as travel tests, tracking filters, meeting places, wildlife corridors, and local-knowledge scenes
closing blockquote that gives a generated ford sign and practical table meaning
generation_guidance: Generate creek fords as practical crossings where depth, current, gravel, wheel ruts, cold feet, and animal behavior decide what happens.
Include respectful room for Wintu or other Native families who know seasonal water and old paths, without inventing ceremonies, sacred details, or made-up language.
Give the table hooks around a wagon stuck midstream, washed-out tracks, deer at dawn, salmon or trout sign, a hidden rider downstream, or a crossing that locals refuse after storm rain.
Make water a clock and a witness: it carries blood, scatters tracks, chills horses, and tells who crossed recently.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: The ford should feel shallow until the wrong wheel drops into the wrong hole.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A creek-ford scene should ask who knows the bottom, what the water erased, and which bank is safer to leave from.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western road, water, and tracking language only.
The generated page must include image path regions/images/creek-fords.png.
The prompt must not contain final prose, final blockquotes, or finished field marks.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not romanticize Native people or invent cultural details
do not use concrete culverts, modern bridges, plastic debris, road signs, or recreational fishing language
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about fords as travel, tracking, water, wildlife, and scene engines.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
